transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. Thomas the Vance here, TV on the radio. Welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And tonight two archive sessions from Bebop Deluxe, the 1974 and 1975 lineups represented.
Also the first of our Friday Rock Show sessions that feature up and coming new bands.
Tonight from Sheffield, Def Leppard.
We've got classic cuts by the Beatles, yes, Jimi Hendrix, Bruce Springsteen and others,
and new releases from Steve Hillage, ACDC, Brand X and Samson.
Not forgetting, of course, our Friday Night Connection, that little teaser that comes your way this time every week.
But this evening, we start with Queen.
Steve Hillage, and a track from his album, which is called Open.
That was the title track.
He's just about to start a big tour of the United Kingdom,
and I met him, what, in the middle of last week?
He really is a nice guy and a great instrumentalist.
And before that, he heard something from the album called News of the World
from the band called Queen, and the track was called Sheer Heart Attack.
And it was for a gentleman by the name of Julian,
with very best wishes from Damien Dunleath, I think it is,
of King's College in Taunton.
I'm told that Julian is suffering at the moment from a bad throat.
I wouldn't worry about it, Julian. I've had one for years.
Also, we played the Queen track for Nathan Webster,
who's told me quite a long, sad story about his family.
All I can say, Nathan, is that I hope things get better.
Seven minutes after ten, and here's the first track
from our heavy new band section.
This is Def Leppard and Rock Brigade.
It came out in 1969, a track from Abbey Road by The Beatles,
the track, Of Course I Want You, She's So Heavy.
And I played that because I got a letter that went like this
from Pete Atkinson of 25 Linden Court, Annerley Road in Londinium.
Dear Tom, this is your final warning to play my request
for something from Abbey Road by the Beatles,
preferably She's So Heavy.
Please dedicate it to Janet Farmer in particular
and all the rock freaks and the hippies
at Langley Park, 6th Form.
Pete, how can I refuse?
Don't forget next week, well, you don't know it yet
because I don't think anybody's told you,
but I'll tell you now, the Beatles are going to be
our archive session next week, so do listen.
That ought to make you even happier, Peter.
Prior to that, you heard Def Leppard
and the first of four from their session tonight.
It was called Rock Brigade.
Recording for the John Peel Show, 9th, 5th, 1974, featuring Bill Nelson, of course,
but also Robert Bryan on bass, Ian Darkin on guitar, and Nick Jew on drums.
Here's Bebop Deluxe.
Again, adventures in a Yorkshire landscape, and Third Floor Heaven, 15th of July,
and Mill Street Junction, one of the early sessions put together by Bebop Deluxe for the BBC.
And that featured the 1974 line-up.
Stay tuned, and we'll have Bill Nelson and the 1975 line-up later on in the programme.
And that, of course, is the line-up that everybody remembers Bebop for in many, many ways.
Many people asked to hear Bebop again on the Friday Rock Show.
Just to name a few, Paul Grunhill of Schoolhouse Islip Kettering in Northampton,
Dave Tranter of 12 Chapster Road Ford Houses in Wolverhampton,
Tony Dehaney of 40 Yarning Gale Road in Coventry, I think it is,
and Dave Pinder of 18 South End at Broadgate in Preston.
AC, DC.
And the girls got rhythm, especially for My Misses.
And that's their new single.
It's also on their album Highway to Hell.
And at the moment, they're tearing the UK apart.
Before that, you heard TV is King.
Thank you very much.
That was by The Tubes, and it was requested by Michael Kenya
of No. 11 Grenville Avenue, Walton, Lidale in Preston.
And he wanted me to give a mention to everybody at Sunderland Polytechnic
and also to Jane, to Beth, to Liz and to Anne.
Four-time winner, Mike.
A quick reminder about our little thing
that we're going to put out on November the 16th
which is called And Now We Are One
because on that day we will be exactly one year of age
here on the Friday Rock Show.
The idea is that you send in your three most favourite
and much-loved rock tracks
and then we take everybody's choice,
put it all together, whack it through the BBC computer
and come out with some sort of chart
that really does reflect exactly the type of music that you like.
now this is your last chance to get in
on this once in a lifetime offer
your three most favourite
much loved rock tracks
on a card, postcard, whatever
and send them to
and now we are one
the Friday Rock Show
BBC Radio 1
London W1A4WW
quarter to eleven
ten minutes and forty seconds long
and delightful to the ear
here's yes and you and I

in leeds he tells me that yesterday was john anderson's birthday mr anderson happy birthday

a lady by the name of Bev Mallett,
who wants me to mention a lot of hard-working people

...and the burning of the Midnight Lamp for Harvey Stone and for Paul and Phil and Wayne and Russ

and any other friends and budding young chemists.
And R. Selwood lives in Princess Gardens, London, SW7.
Round the corner, ain't he?
Also, we played that for Spider of Peterhead in Bonny, Scotland.
And prior to that, you heard the single that's coming out by Def Leppard.
Actually, it's out at the moment.
Just to let you know, Def Leppard, R, Joe Elliott on vocals, Pete Willis on guitar,
Steve Clark on guitar, Rick Savage on bass, and Rick Allen on drums.
And they're currently on tour with ACDC.
You can get a ticket. You can get in to see them.
27th, they're in Glasgow. 29th in Manchester during the month of November.
1st to the 4th, Hammersmith Odeon, I think it is.
5th and 6th in Liverpool. 8th, Stafford.
The 9th, Leicester.
And Def Leppard are going to have an album out in February.
Four minutes after 11, and it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
Last week, we played you Built for Comfort by UFO.
I just want to make love to you by Foggart and Little Red Rooster by The Rolling Stones.
Now, the connection, well, not too many people, that is, seem to know what the connection was at all.
But a number of people knew what the music was, but they didn't really know what the connection was.
The connection was that all the songs were written by that great old blues geezer by the name of Willie Dixon.
Willie Dixon, all right? Are you new?
Anyway, the winner has got a £10 record token, or will have within a couple of days,
as Mr. G. Mills of 74 Scrimshaw House, Oxford Street, Plek in Walsall.
Mr. Mills, congratulations.
£10 record token is definitely on its way.
Once I can get the money out of T. Wilson, which, as we all know by now, is desperately hard, the old tightwad.
But I must say that to Mr. G. Mills has put together a really nice Friday night connection.
Somewhere in these three bits of music, there is a connection.
Well, I think it's safe to say that difficult it really isn't this week.
Somewhere in those three bits of music, there's a connection.
Simple, simple.
You think you know what it is?
Jot it down on a postcard, in a letter.
Anything you like, really, send it along to the Friday Night Connection.

Don't forget to put together your own Friday Night Connection as well.

Bruce Springsteen, whose name I always find it very hard to say, and the track called Sandy.
